description_long: This is a portable metal crowd control barrier, commonly seen in urban environments for managing pedestrian flow or cordoning off restricted areas. The structure consists of a series of evenly spaced vertical bars welded to a single horizontal rail at the top and another at the bottom, forming a rigid rectangular frame. The ends of the barrier curve gently outward, connecting to angled support legs that provide stability and prevent tipping. The entire frame appears to be constructed from tubular steel, giving it a robust and industrial appearance. The surface is finished in a dark, matte gray, likely powder-coated for weather resistance. There are no visible decorative elements, caps, or gates, emphasizing its utilitarian function.
description: A sturdy, portable metal crowd control barrier with vertical bars and angled support legs, finished in matte gray. Designed for urban crowd management, it features a simple, utilitarian style.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the barrier, with evenly spaced vertical bars and both support legs visible.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the barrier and the Y-shaped configuration of the support legs.
description_view_2: Rear view, visually identical to the front, displaying the vertical bars and support legs.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the narrow profile and angled support legs.
